text.title: Authorization and Query Limits
text.summary: Permission classes for field/mutation auth, query depth limiter, complexity extension, pagination caps.

rule.statement: Apply authorization at the field resolver level using permission classes, not only at the gateway or query root. A field added to a type inherits no auth from its parent.
rule.rationale: GraphQL composition allows clients to embed any field in any query. A gateway-level auth check on the top-level query does not propagate down to nested fields added later.

rule.statement: Add query depth limiting (max 10) and complexity analysis before production. Set a hard max on pagination `first` arguments server-side.
rule.rationale: A `friends.friends.friends...` query triggers exponential DB calls. Without server-side pagination caps, a client requesting `first: 1000000` bypasses the design and runs a full table scan.

example.code:
from strawberry.extensions import QueryDepthLimiter from strawberry.extensions.query_complexity import QueryComplexityExtension
schema = strawberry.Schema( query=Query, mutation=Mutation, extensions=[ QueryDepthLimiter(max_depth=10), QueryComplexityExtension(max_complexity=1000), ], )
# Server-side pagination cap in resolver @strawberry.field async def users(self, info: Info, first: int = 20) -> UserConnection: if first > 100: raise ValueError("Cannot request more than 100 items") ...
